# Preserving your work

Save a copy of files you create during this task to `/logs/artifacts/`. This directory is preserved after the run so outputs can be inspected. Organize with subdirectories if helpful (e.g., `/logs/artifacts/code/`, `/logs/artifacts/reports/`). The directory already exists and is writable.

---

# Full Blog Api With Relations

Build a complete blog REST API using Prisma ORM and Express.js with SQLite.

The project is at `/home/user/myproject`. Node.js and Express are pre-installed.

Define the following schema with migrations:
- `User`: id, email (unique), name
- `Post`: id, title, content (optional), published Boolean @default(false), authorId (FK to User), createdAt DateTime @default(now())
- `Comment`: id, body, postId (FK to Post), authorId (FK to User)

Build an Express server at `server.js` on port 3000 with these endpoints:
- `POST /users` — create user
- `GET /users/:id/posts` — get published posts by user (include comment count)
- `POST /posts` — create post (body: `{ title, content, authorId }`)
- `PATCH /posts/:id/publish` — set `published: true`
- `POST /posts/:id/comments` — add a comment (body: `{ body, authorId }`)
- `GET /posts/:id` — get post with author and comments included

Start the server: `node server.js`
Port: 3000
Project path: `/home/user/myproject`

# Preserving your work

Save a copy of files you create during this task to `/logs/artifacts/`. This directory is preserved after the run so outputs can be inspected. Organize with subdirectories if helpful (e.g., `/logs/artifacts/code/`, `/logs/artifacts/reports/`). The directory already exists and is writable.

   - POST /posts/:id/comments — add a comment (body: { body, authorId })
   - GET /posts/:id — get post with author and comments included

   - Post: id, title, content (optional), published Boolean @default(false), authorId (FK to User), createdAt DateTime @default(now())
   - Comment: id, body, postId (FK to Post), authorId (FK to User)

2. Express server at server.js on port 3000 with endpoints:
   - POST /users — create user
   - GET /users/:id/posts — get published posts by user (include comment count)
   - POST /posts — create post (body: { title, content, authorId })
   - PATCH /posts/:id/publish — set published: true
   - POST /posts/:id/comments — add a comment (body: { body, authorId })
   - GET /posts/:id — get post with author and comments included

3. Start the server with `node server.js`

input.result:
## Blog REST API Successfully Built and Running! 🚀

I've successfully built a complete blog REST API using Prisma ORM v7 and Express.js with SQLite. The server is now running on port 3000.

### What Was Created:

#### 1. **Database Schema** (`prisma/schema.prisma`)
- **User**: id, email (unique), name
- **Post**: id, title, content (optional), published Boolean @default(false), authorId (FK to User), createdAt DateTime @default(now())
- **Comment**: id, body, postId (FK to Post), authorId (FK to User)

#### 2. **API Endpoints** (`server.js`)
All endpoints are fully implemented with proper error handling:

- **POST /users** — Create a user
  - Body: `{ email, name }`
  - Returns: Created user object

- **GET /users/:id/posts** — Get published posts by a user with comment count
  - Returns: User info and array of published posts with comment counts

- **POST /posts** — Create a post
  - Body: `{ title, content, authorId }`
  - Returns: Created post object

- **PATCH /posts/:id/publish** — Publish a post
  - Sets `published: true`
  - Returns: Updated post object

- **POST /posts/:id/comments** — Add a comment to a post
  - Body: `{ body, authorId }`
  - Returns: Created comment object

- **GET /posts/:id** — Get a post with author and comments
  - Returns: Full post object with nested author and comments data

#### 3. **Technical Implementation**
- ✅ Prisma 7 with @prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3 for SQLite support
- ✅ Express.js with JSON middleware
- ✅ Proper error handling and validation
- ✅ Cascade delete relationships
- ✅ Graceful shutdown handling
- ✅ Database migrations applied

#### 4. **Artifacts Saved**
All key files have been saved to `/logs/artifacts/code/`:
- `schema.prisma` - Database schema
- `server.js` - Express server with all endpoints
- `migrations/` - Database migration files

### Server Status:
✅ **Server is running** on http://localhost:3000

You can now test the API endpoints using curl, Postman, or any HTTP client!
